methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
GHGSat is a Seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Stephane Germain that has raised $67M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a fragmented comp universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Planet Labs, though narrow-stage comparables remain sparse.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using $10M as a proxy for the company's implied post-seed baseline valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier standard to the Seed stage yields an implied valuation of $25M. The Peer-Median approach is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the Space & Aerospace sector—while 25 total peers exist in the sector, none of the narrow filters produced the required 3+ datapoints. Public-Comp anchoring cannot be applied because GHGSat's revenue is not disclosed, preventing use of the sector's 42.9x median P/S multiple across the 5 public comps.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median point of $25M. This range reflects the mechanical output of a single method applied to limited inputs. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer multiples at the Seed stage and lack of disclosed revenue metrics, which limits the precision of this estimate and argues for additional diligence on GHGSat's unit economics and customer contracts.
